cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what upset Melanie during the National Championships. The passage mentions that she entered the National Championships but came nowhere, despite having the second fastest swim and fastest run. Then it says, "I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realised then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running." So, the problem here was that she made a mistake during part of the race—specifically, she took a wrong turn or went the wrong way during the cycling part, which made her cycle 20 km too far. That would have cost her a lot of time and energy, leading to her poor performance. Looking at the answer choices: A) She was tricked by another competitor. But the passage says she was following a man, not that he tricked her. It seems like they both went wrong, not that he intentionally misled her. B) She felt she had let her teammates down. The text doesn't mention teammates in this context. She was in an individual race, so this seems unlikely. C) She made a mistake during part of
